- Step 23: top purple, bottom white
So painting the bottom rectangle purple didn't work - it went back to purple on top instead. Wait, let me look more carefully. The bottom rectangle is still white. The top one is purple. It seems like the bottom paint action didn't work as expected.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
rects = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 50 and n['color'] in ('W', 'p', 'P'):
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [b[0] for b in boundary]
        cols = [b[1] for b in boundary]
        center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        rects.append((center_row, n['color'], n['pixels']))
        print(f"  center=({center_row}, {center_col}), color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
rects.sort()
print(f"Top rect: {rects[0]}")
print(f"Bottom rect: {rects[1]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
